response.overview: In a city where everyone seems to be surviving more than living, *People Are Made of Metal* explores the hidden sides of human nature through moments of tension, silence, and unexpected empathy. The film follows ordinary people placed in ordinary situations, yet every reaction reveals something deeper about who they really are when pressure takes over. Built around raw conversations, uncomfortable emotions, and the contrast between harshness and humanity, the film reflects on how people can break, harden, or heal depending on what life throws at them. Some carry kindness quietly. Others hide pain behind anger. And sometimes, the smallest moment is enough to expose the real “metal” inside a person. A personal and grounded short film inspired by the streets, emotions, and contradictions we pass by every day.
response.tagline: Pressure reveals what people are truly made of.
response.title: El Nas Maaden
